summary:
Alkaline phosphatase and pyrophosphatase regulate critical aspects of mineral metabolism essential for bone formation; their dysregulation can lead to various pathological conditions such as calciphylaxis and vascular calcifications observed clinically in patients suffering from renal failure among other disorders affecting skeletal health maintenance through enzymatic control over ion homeostasis during calcification processes.
